tribe_v2_insight: Perceived generosity activates reward pathways in the striatum, creating an implicit sense of social obligation
user_explanation: This triggers reciprocity — the social obligation to return a favour. When someone gives you something (even unsolicited), your brain's reward pathways create pressure to reciprocate, which can be exploited to drive actions you wouldn't otherwise take.
legal_note: Reciprocity tactics combined with hidden obligations may constitute unfair commercial practices under the EU Unfair Commercial Practices Directive.
